feedback_addressed: Added comprehensive commodity sensitivity tables for FCX (copper $3.50-$5.65/lb), NUE (HRC $650-$1,100/ton), ALB (lithium $10-$30/kg), NEM (gold $2,500-$5,000/oz), LIN (volume scenarios), CRH (infrastructure scenarios)
Added 2 SELL recommendations: MP Materials (unprofitable, -$0.22 EPS, reclassified from BUY) and ALB (realistic EPS $4-$5 vs consensus $7.87, lithium supply overhang)
Reconciled copper supply-demand contradiction: Goldman surplus vs ICSG deficit explicitly compared with base case of balanced market +/-50kt

Added cycle positioning analysis for copper (late), steel (policy-driven mid), lithium (early recovery), gold (late/momentum), construction materials (mid with fiscal tailwind), industrial gases (through-cycle)
Added position sizing reflecting commodity cyclicality: LIN 6%, CRH 5%, FCX 3.5%, NUE 2.5%, NEM 2.0%, ALB/MP 0%
Added cost curve analysis for FCX (Grasberg -$0.92/lb, Americas $2.45/lb) and NEM ($1,680/oz AISC, upper quartile)
Added LIN historical PE analysis: 33.5x is BELOW 10-year median of 33.85x, answering v1 question about whether premium is justified
Added CRH acquisition ROIC analysis: improved from 8.42% to 11.87%, confirming value creation
Added NUE tariff dependency analysis with 4 reversion scenarios and probability-weighted expected EPS of $8.65
Added ALB DLE technology analysis: pilot phase, no commercial scale economics, earliest deployment 2028-2029
Added Chinese rare earth impact on MP: restrictions suspended until Nov 2026, removing near-term catalyst
Added FCX Grasberg political risk: Indonesian royalty increases, 51% local ownership requirement, mud rush disruption
Downgraded NUE from BUY to HOLD, NEM from BUY to HOLD
Exposed ALB 1,096% growth as misleading base-effect distortion from $0.66 adjusted EPS
remaining_flaws: NEM HOLD at 2% weight with gold at $4,500+ is a position that benefits primarily in scenarios where the rest of the portfolio is declining -- the portfolio construction logic for this hedge position could be more precisely articulated
FCX position at 3.5% acknowledges late-cycle risk but the Grasberg mud rush recovery to only 85% in H2 2026 adds operational risk on top of commodity risk -- could warrant further reduction
The sector weight of 19% vs 2.5% XLB benchmark is a significant overweight that rests heavily on the infrastructure spending thesis; if IIJA disbursements disappoint, the overweight is exposed
what_impressed: MP Materials reclassification from BUY to SELL with the explicit statement that an unprofitable $8.5B company cannot receive the same rating as $220B Linde is exactly the correction demanded
ALB SELL with exposure of the 1,096% growth figure as base-effect distortion and realistic EPS of $4-$5 at current lithium prices demonstrates analytical integrity
Copper supply-demand reconciliation citing Goldman, ICSG, JP Morgan, and World Bank with balanced assessment is the objective analysis v1 lacked
NUE tariff dependency analysis with probability-weighted expected EPS ($8.65) vs current ($11) showing limited upside is genuine risk management
LIN PE analysis discovering that 33.5x is actually below the 10-year median contradicts the v1 premise that LIN was expensive -- intellectual honesty in reversing a prior assumption
CRH ROIC improvement from 8.42% to 11.87% directly answering whether acquisitions create or destroy value
Gold macro paradox note (gold >$4,500 implies equity decline 20-30%) shows sophisticated portfolio-level thinking
